mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and other kinases to suppress FLT3-ITD–driven signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Alternative name: Rydapt; described as FLT3/multi-kinase; suggested due to FLT3-ITD dependency in MOLM-13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and AXL to block oncogenic signaling in FLT3-driven AML
additional_notes: Alternative name: Xospata; noted as strong FLT3-ITD inhibitor class

mechanism: Selective inhibition of FLT3 signaling to reduce proliferation in FLT3-dependent AML cells
additional_notes: Text notes you may extend to 10 µM if needed

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 and RAF to suppress proliferative signaling pathways
additional_notes: Mentioned as off-label in FLT3-ITD AML contexts; multi-kinase incl. FLT3/RAF

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family and ABL kinases affecting AML signaling dependencies and survival pathways
additional_notes: Noted as sometimes synergizing with other agents

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway downstream of FLT3, reducing proliferation
additional_notes: Described as targeting MAPK pathway downstream of FLT3

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 to dampen PI3K/AKT/mTOR-driven growth and survival signaling
additional_notes: Highlights PI3K/AKT/mTOR axis relevance in AML

mechanism: Selective inhibition of PI3K delta to reduce PI3K signaling implicated in hematologic malignancies
additional_notes: Target specified as PI3Kδ

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, altering chromatin/transcription to suppress AML growth and sensitize to other drugs
additional_notes: Alternative name: SAHA; noted as HDAC inhibitor (HDACi) with sensitization potential

mechanism: Potent HDAC inhibition leading to transcriptional dysregulation and antiproliferative effects in AML cell lines
additional_notes: Text notes activity often low-nM to sub-µM; may extend to 10 µM

mechanism: Inhibits class I HDACs to modulate gene expression and inhibit AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Specifically noted as Class I HDAC targeting

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases, causing hypomethylation and growth inhibition (time-dependent)
additional_notes: Standard AML drug; text suggests 72–120 h and refresh dosing considerations

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA/DNA and reduces methylation via DNMT inhibition, affecting gene expression and viability with longer exposure
additional_notes: Standard AML drug; noted that in vitro effects often require extended exposure

mechanism: Activates retinoic acid signaling to drive differentiation and alter proliferation programs in myeloid cells
additional_notes: Alternative name/acronym: ATRA; noted not limited to APL in vitro

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 to trigger mitochondrial apoptosis in susceptible AML cells
additional_notes: Described as backbone of modern AML therapy; noted as often very potent (include low nM)

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome causing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Text notes you may extend to 10 µM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome leading to proteostasis disruption and cell death
additional_notes: Text notes you may extend to 10 µM; mentioned as sometimes having distinct sensitivity profiles

mechanism: Impacts mitochondrial function and activates AMPK-related metabolic stress pathways to reduce proliferation in some AML contexts
additional_notes: Noted as usually requiring higher µM–mM concentrations in vitro; described as mitochondrial/AMPK effects

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial electron transport chain to impair cellular respiration and growth
additional_notes: Described as antimalarial; mitochondrial ETC inhibition rationale

mechanism: Reported to have multiple anticancer effects leading to antiproliferative activity in some leukemia/cancer models
additional_notes: Described as antiparasitic; mechanism noted as 'multiple reported anticancer effects' in models incl. leukemia
